name: MITRE.LIN.T1072.RULE Software Deployment Tools
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

# RPM (Redhat/CentOS)
-w /usr/bin/rpm -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /usr/bin/yum -p x -k siem-package-manager

# YAST/Zypper/RPM (SuSE)
-w /sbin/yast -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /sbin/yast2 -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /bin/rpm -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /usr/bin/zypper -k siem-package-manager

# DPKG / APT-GET (Debian/Ubuntu)
-w /usr/bin/dpkg -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /usr/bin/apt-add-repository -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /usr/bin/apt-get -p x -k siem-package-manager
-w /usr/bin/aptitude -p x -k siem-package-manager

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-package-manager
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1072.RULE Software Deployment Tools on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-package-manager
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1072.RULE Software Deployment Tools

Event description: Adversaries may gain access to and use third-party systems installed within an enterprise network, such as administration, monitoring, and deployment systems as well as third-party gateways and jump servers used for managing other systems.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1072
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
